channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: New Jersey proposes rules requiring data centers to cover their own energy and infrastructure costs
item.description: New Jersey Governor Mikie Sherrill has unveiled a new policy proposal aimed at changing how data centers operate in the state, particularly in relation to their energy consumption and impact on public...

item.title: President Ruto mourns Utumishi Girls Academy dormitory fire victims as rescue and medical response continues
item.description: President William Ruto has expressed deep sorrow following a tragic fire incident at Utumishi Girls Academy in Gilgil, where students lost their lives after a dormitory blaze. The Head of State descri...

item.title: Environmental groups raise concerns over proposed P26-billion waste-to-energy plant in Smokey Mountain, Manila
item.description: Residents of Smokey Mountain in Tondo, Manila are facing renewed concerns over a proposed waste-to-energy project valued at around P26.6 billion, which aims to convert solid waste into electricity. Th...

item.title: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah Announces Resignation to Make Way for New Leadership
item.description: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah, a veteran Congress leader with nearly five decades in politics, informed his Cabinet colleagues of his decision to resign during a breakfast meeting at his offic...

item.title: Comelec advises voters on how to respond to summons regarding voter application challenges
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) in the Philippines has issued guidance for individuals who receive summons related to their voter registration applications. These summons are issued by the Elect...

item.title: Understanding Infrastructure Bonds and Their Investment Value in Tanzania
item.description: The article discusses the growing attention around infrastructure bonds in Tanzania following the Budget Speech by the Minister for Works, which was accompanied by media headlines suggesting the gover...

item.title: Concerns grow over potential insider trading in oil markets amid US-Israeli conflict with Iran
item.description: Well-founded concerns have emerged regarding suspicious trading activities in oil markets, particularly during the recent US-Israeli conflict with Iran. The Trump administration reportedly issued a st...

item.title: Calgary council delays decision on free CTrain fare zone amid safety debate
item.description: Calgary city council has voted to delay any final decision on the future of the downtown free fare zone on the CTrain system, pushing the discussion to early 2027 as part of a broader review of transi...

item.title: Gray Malin’s ‘Art of Living’ Series Places Mid-Century Furniture on Floating Platforms in Bora Bora
item.description: The article showcases a visual art project by photographer Gray Malin titled “Art of Living,” which continues his exploration of playful, stylized environments. In this series, Malin stages mid-centur...

item.title: Jetour T2 becomes first Chinese vehicle to win South African Car of the Year
item.description: The Jetour T2 has made history by becoming the first Chinese vehicle to win the South African Car of the Year 2026. Since its arrival in South Africa in November 2025, the T2 has quickly become a favo...

item.title: Ethiopia 2026 elections: ruling party seeks legitimacy amid conflict and national fragmentation
item.description: Ethiopia’s general election scheduled for 1 June 2026 is happening at a very tense and divided period for the country, as multiple conflicts and political fragmentation continue to shape national life...

item.title: Kenya school dormitory fire claims 16 lives and leaves 73 hospitalised
item.description: A devastating fire at a girls’ boarding school in Kenya has resulted in the deaths of 16 students and left 73 others hospitalised. The blaze broke out around 1.00 am local time on Thursday at Utumishi...

item.title: Government plans clarifications to encryption and data retention rules in Bill C-22
item.description: The Liberal government, led by Public Safety Minister Gary Anandasangaree, has announced plans to amend Bill C-22 following widespread criticism from privacy advocates and tech companies. The bill, wh...

item.title: Kanyari reveals daughter Sky Victor earns KSh 500,000 monthly from advertising work in maize milling company
item.description: Controversial Kenyan preacher Pastor Kanyari has claimed that his daughter, Sky Victor, is earning KSh 500,000 every month through an advertising job linked to a maize milling company. He made the rem...

item.title: Rare blue micromoon to be visible across Australia on Sunday night, peaking at 6:45 pm
item.description: Australians are set to witness a rare lunar event this Sunday night, known as a blue micromoon, which combines two separate astronomical phenomena: a blue moon and a micromoon. Despite its name, the m...

item.title: Isah Ashiru emerges Kaduna ADC governorship candidate amid Sani’s rejection
item.description: Former House of Representatives member, Isah Ashiru, don finally emerge as the governorship candidate for African Democratic Congress (ADC) for Kaduna State after the party primary. Ashiru wey once re...

item.title: Tanzania Seeks Diplomatic Engagement with U.S. Senators Over Proposed Sanctions Legislation
item.description: Tanzania’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs and East African Cooperation has extended an official invitation to members of the United States Senate to visit the country as part of ongoing diplomatic effort...

item.title: Supreme Court recognises trauma care as part of right to life, orders overhaul of emergency response system
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has delivered a landmark judgment recognising access to trauma care as an essential component of the Right to Life under Article 21 of the Constitution. In its order in the ...

item.title: Letters to the editor debate pipelines, federal unity, and regional tensions over energy policy in Canada
item.description: This collection of letters to the editor reflects a range of perspectives on Canadian federal politics, energy infrastructure, and regional tensions, particularly between Alberta, British Columbia, an...

item.title: President Marcos Jr delivers address before Japan’s National Diet during state visit
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. delivered an address before Japan’s National Diet on Thursday, May 28, 2026, as part of his four-day state visit to Japan. The National Diet address is considered a rare...
